audit_status.source_base: Greek Philippians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for warning, confidence in flesh, loss/gain, knowing Messiah, righteousness from trust, pressing toward goal, heavenly citizenship.

verses.restored: Finally, my siblings, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you is not troublesome for me, and for you it is safe.
Watch the dogs; watch the harmful workers; watch the mutilation.
For we are the circumcision, the ones serving by Spirit of the Divine and boasting in Messiah Jesus and not having confidence in flesh,
though I myself have confidence even in flesh. If any other thinks to have confidence in flesh, I more:
circumcised on the eighth day, from the race of Israel, tribe of Benjamin, Hebrew of Hebrews; according to Law, Pharisee;
according to zeal, pursuing/persecuting the assembly; according to righteousness/justice in Law, having become blameless.
But whatever things were gains to me, these I have considered loss because of Messiah.

Indeed therefore I also consider all things to be loss because of the surpassing value of the knowledge of Messiah Jesus my Lord, because of whom I lost all things and consider them refuse, so that I may gain Messiah
and be found in him, not having my own righteousness/justice from Law, but that through trust/faithfulness of Messiah, the righteousness/justice from the Divine upon trust,
to know him and the power of his resurrection and the fellowship/participation of his sufferings, being conformed to his death,
if somehow I may arrive into the resurrection from the dead.
Not that I already received or already have been made complete, but I pursue, if indeed I may take hold of that for which I also was taken hold of by Messiah Jesus.
Siblings, I do not calculate myself to have taken hold; but one thing: forgetting the things behind and stretching forward to the things ahead,
I pursue toward the goal for the prize of the upward calling of the Divine in Messiah Jesus.
Therefore, as many as are mature/complete, let us think this; and if in anything you think otherwise, this also the Divine will unveil to you.
Only, to what we have reached, walk in line by the same.
Become co-imitators of me, siblings, and watch those walking this way, just as you have us as pattern.
For many walk, of whom I often told you and now tell you weeping, as enemies of the cross of Messiah,
whose completion is destruction, whose god is the belly, and whose glory is in their shame, those thinking earthly things.
For our citizenship exists in heavens, from where also we eagerly await a savior/healer, Lord Jesus Messiah,
who will transform the body of our humiliation to be conformed to the body of his glory, according to the working by which he is able even to subject all things to himself.

translation_notes: Paul's sharp warning in 3:2 targets rival confidence-in-flesh pressure, not Jewish identity as contempt. His own Jewish credentials are named as real but relativized in Messiah.
Righteousness/justice through trust/faithfulness of Messiah preserves pistis Christou ambiguity.
The body is transformed, not discarded; 3:21 is embodied hope.
